sect1.title: Introduction
sect1.para: The MetricTemplate class, implemented in Cython, serves as a robust and high-performance base for defining and calculating various metrics within the AnalysisG framework. It is specifically designed to handle the complex task of interpreting and processing data from ROOT files, often generated in high-energy physics experiments. This class provides mechanisms for managing run names, variables, and a sophisticated InterpretROOT method that leverages parallel processing for efficient data extraction and metric computation.

sect1.title: The MetricTemplate Class
sect1.para: MetricTemplate is a cdef class, indicating its nature as a Cython extension type. This allows for direct and optimized interaction with underlying C++ data structures and algorithms, crucial for performance-intensive tasks like ROOT file I/O and data processing. It encapsulates a C++ metric_template object and provides a Python-friendly interface to its functionalities.

listitem.para: __cinit__(self): The Cython "constructor" equivalent, called during object allocation. It initializes a new C++ metric_template object and assigns its pointer to self.mtx. It also initializes self.root_leaves and self.root_fx as empty Python dictionaries, which are used to configure ROOT file interpretation.
__init__(self): The standard Python initializer, intentionally left empty as primary initialization is handled by __cinit__.
__dealloc__(self): The Cython "destructor" equivalent, called upon object deallocation. It deallocates the C++ metric_template object pointed to by self.mtx, ensuring proper memory cleanup.
__name__(self): Returns the name of the underlying C++ metric object as a Python string, using the env utility function for conversion.

sect2.title: Properties
sect2.para: These properties provide controlled access to the configuration and state of the MetricTemplate.

listitem.para:
@property RunNames: Retrieves a dictionary of run names (C++ map<string, string>) associated with the metric. The C++ map is converted to a Python dictionary using as_basic_dict.
@RunNames.setter RunNames(self, dict val): Sets the run names. The input Python dictionary val is converted to a C++ map<string, string> using as_map and assigned to self.mtx.run_names.

@property Variables: Retrieves a list of variables (C++ vector<string>) managed by the metric. The C++ vector is converted to a Python list using env_vec.
@Variables.setter Variables(self, list val): Sets the list of variables. The input Python list val is converted to a C++ vector<string> using enc_list and assigned to self.mtx.variables.
Postprocessing(self): An empty placeholder method intended to be overridden by subclasses for any post-processing steps required after metric calculation.

InterpretROOT(self, str path, list epochs = [], list kfolds = []): This is the core method for interpreting and processing data from ROOT files. It orchestrates the reading of ROOT files, filtering by epochs and k-folds, and applying user-defined functions (root_fx) to extracted data.
Parameters:
path (str): The path to the ROOT file(s). Can be a directory, a specific .root file, or a path ending with / (which implies /*).
epochs (list, optional): A list of epoch numbers to filter the ROOT files. Defaults to an empty list (no filtering).
kfolds (list, optional): A list of k-fold numbers to filter the ROOT files. Defaults to an empty list (no filtering).
Functionality:
Configuration Check: Verifies that self.root_leaves (a dictionary mapping tree names to lists of leaves) and self.root_fx (a dictionary mapping tree/leaf names to functions) are properly set. If not, it logs failure messages and returns self.
Path Normalization: Adjusts the path argument to ensure it's suitable for file globbing (e.g., adds * if it's a directory).
File Filtering: Uses self.ptr.ls (a C++ function) to list ROOT files and then filters them based on provided epochs and kfolds. prange is used for parallel processing of file paths.
Data Structure Initialization: Initializes various C++ maps and vectors (idx_map, endx, pause, lsx, mxf, mapfx, mapdx, mapx) to manage file indexing, processing state, and data mapping.
Leaf and Function Mapping: Iterates through self.root_leaves and self.root_fx to build internal mappings (mapfx, mapdx) that link ROOT tree/leaf combinations to their corresponding processing functions.
ROOT I/O Setup: Initializes an IO object (presumably from AnalysisG.core.io) with the filtered ROOT file paths. It configures the IO object with the Trees and Leaves to be read based on self.root_leaves.
Iterative Data Processing Loop:
Enters a while keep_going loop to iteratively read and process data from the ROOT files.
Metric Function Execution: For each configured root_fx, it gathers data (itx) and calls the associated function, passing self, the extracted data, and metadata (epoch, kfold, filename).
File Advancement: Uses prange to advance the internal pointers of the data_t objects (representing entries in ROOT trees) for all active files.
Metadata Extraction: Extracts metadata like kfold, epoch, and model_name from the current file path.
Progress Reporting: Updates a progress bar (iox.prg) with the current file being processed.
Pause/Unpause Logic: Manages pause and endx flags to control which files are actively being read and when a file has been fully processed.
Loop Termination: The loop continues as long as keep_going is true, which is set to false when all files have been fully processed.
Postprocessing Call: After the main processing loop, it calls self.Postprocessing().
Return Value: Returns self for method chaining.

sect1.title: C++ Interaction
sect1.para:
MetricTemplate extensively uses Cython's capabilities to bridge Python and C++:
cdef class MetricTemplate: Declares it as a Cython extension type.
cdef metric_template *mtx: A C++ pointer to an metric_template object.
from libcpp cimport string, map, pair, vector: Imports C++ standard library types.
from AnalysisG.core.tools cimport *, from AnalysisG.core.meta cimport *, from AnalysisG.core.structs cimport *: Imports C++ declarations from corresponding .pxd files.
cython.parallel.prange: Enables parallel execution of loops, leveraging multiple CPU cores for faster processing.
cython.operator.dereference as deref: Used to dereference C++ pointers.
env, enc, env_vec, enc_list, as_basic_dict, as_map: Utility functions (likely from AnalysisG.core.tools) for converting between Python and C++ data types (strings, lists, dictionaries, vectors, maps).
data_t* dt: Pointer to a C++ data_t structure, likely representing a single entry from a ROOT tree.
IO iox: An instance of a Cython-wrapped C++ I/O class for ROOT file handling.
